response.justification: The candidate is an experienced full-stack/web engineer, but the profile fundamentally mismatches this embedded networking security role. There is no evidence of native-level Japanese proficiency (a hard requirement), no Linux kernel investigation or modification experience, and only brief C/C++ exposure (6 months at National Instruments) versus the required strong C-for-UNIX proficiency. None of the core security technologies (MACsec, 802.1X, tamper detection) or L2/L3 switch development experience appear in the resume.
strengths: Long, varied engineering career with low-latency systems work (DLang, async I/O) at Dunnhumby
Some C/C++ experience on developer tooling at National Instruments
Strong software craftsmanship practices (TDD, CI/CD) and open-source contributions
concerns: No Japanese language ability listed — a mandatory requirement for the role
No embedded firmware, Linux kernel, or LAN-switch/networking-protocol development experience
C experience is minimal and dated; career is centered on web/TypeScript/Node.js stacks
